title: Heap Allocation Contracts and Failure Handling · C Contract Brief: Zero Size Policy
summary: A batch importer assumes allocation succeeds and treats zero-byte requests differently across supported C libraries. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem zero size policy increment in allocation wrapper with explicit outcomes. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose and test a project policy for zero elements rather than depending on an implementation-specific returned pointer.
Propagate allocation failure without partial-state leaks, null dereferences, or impossible-error assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Zero Size Policy · Theory Zero Size Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 9048. Produce bounded C evidence to handle allocation failure and zero-size policy explicitly; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose and test a project policy for zero elements rather than depending on an implementation-specific returned pointer.
